'''Mitchell Allgood''' (born 27 April 1989) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship|Betfred Championship]].

He previously played for the [[Parramatta Eels]] and the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], and [[Hull Kingston Rovers|Hull KR]] and [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]]. He has also played for [[City New South Wales rugby league team|NSW City]] in 2012.

==Background==
Allgood was born in [[Sydney]], [[New South Wales]], [[Australia]].

==Rugby league career==
===Early playing career===
Allgood played his junior football with Brothers, Penrith Junior Rugby League Club. He played in the SG Ball competition with the [[Penrith Panthers]], before signing with Parramatta to play in the [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|Toyota Cup]]. In 2008, Allgood signed a two-year deal with Parramatta after great form in the [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|Toyota Cup]].

===2011-2014: Parramatta Eels===
Allgood made his NRL début in round 1 2011 against the [[New Zealand Warriors]]. He only missed 1 game in his rookie year. He was also named the [[2011 Parramatta Eels season]]'s rookie of the year.
.In 2011, Allgood re-signed with Parramatta until 2013.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.parraeels.com.au/default.aspx?s=player-profile-display&id=255&name=Mitchell%20Allgood&team=Eels|title=Player Profile – Mitchell Allgood|website=Parramatta Eels|publisher=Telstra Media|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110407085611/http://www.parraeels.com.au/default.aspx?s=player-profile-display&id=255&name=Mitchell%20Allgood&team=Eels|archive-date=2011-04-07|url-status=dead}}</ref> In 2013, Allgood was sin binned after punching Manly player [[Steve Matai]] and later suspended for two games.  Allgood claims he was challenged by Matai on the field and said it was a case of "hit or be hit".<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/fear-forced-me-to-hit-steve-matai-says-parramatta-eels-prop-mitchell-allgood/news-story/102de2ccbff570d136e3bf1deb909b24|title=Fear forced me to hit Steve Matai, says Parramatta Eels prop Mitchell Allgood|last=Massoud|first=Josh|date=2015-07-25|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney|access-date=2018-07-20}}</ref>

In September 2013, Allgood was pulled over by police and charged with drink driving.  It was alleged Allgood had been drinking with other Parramatta players as part of the club's [[Mad Monday]] celebrations.  On 18 December 2013, Allgood was handed a two-year good behaviour bond by the magistrate.<ref name="ABC News">{{Cite news|url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2013-09-23/allgood-pleads-guilty-to-drink-driving-charge/4975784|title=Allgood pleads guilty to drink-driving charge|date=2013-09-23|publisher=ABC News|access-date=2018-07-20|language=en-AU}}</ref><ref name="The Daily Telegraph">{{Cite news|url=https://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/mitch-allgood-cops-two-year-good-behaviour-bond-over-mad-monday-drink-driving-charge/news-story/476fd6412812199e013882181540d6ae|title=Mitch Allgood cops two year good behaviour bond over Mad Monday drink driving charge|date=2013-12-18|work=The Daily Telegraph|location=London|access-date=2018-07-20}}</ref>

On 2 June 2014 Allgood was suspended for two games after being found guilty of a dangerous throw on Penrith player [[Josh Mansour]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/eels/mitchell-allgood-cops-two-week-ban-for-dangerous-throw-mitch-rein-accepts-one-week-ban/news-story/bc36a5b55da480ebc034af9ce38ebbeb|title=Mitchell Allgood cops two week ban for dangerous throw, Mitch Rein accepts one week ban|last=Glover|first=Ben|date=2014-06-02|publisher=Fox Sports|access-date=2018-07-20}}</ref>

===2015-2016 Hull Kingston Rovers ===
On 22 November 2014, Allgood signed a three-year contract with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], starting from 2015.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.nrl.com/allgood-joins-hull-kr-in-super-league/tabid/10874/newsid/83096/default.aspx?cid=NRL_HP_Latest|title=Allgood joins Hull KR in Super League|date=2014-11-22|publisher=National Rugby League|access-date=2018-07-20|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141129040517/https://www.nrl.com/allgood-joins-hull-kr-in-super-league/tabid/10874/newsid/83096/default.aspx?cid=NRL_HP_Latest|archive-date=2014-11-29|url-status=dead|agency=Australian Associated Press}}</ref>

===2017-  Wakefield Trinity ===
On 4 December 2016, Allgood signed for Wakefield Trinity on a 1-year deal following the relegation of [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]. Allgood was given the jersey number 10 by [[Chris Chester (rugby league)|Chris Chester]] & [[John Kear]]

===2018-2019 St George Illawarra Dragons===
On 6 November 2017, Allgood signed for the [[St George Illawarra Dragons]] on a one-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/dragons/st-george-illawarra-make-double-depth-signing-complete-2018-roster/news-story/c29df99ed819d24d0f0a9d00010e9b4a|title=St George Illawarra make double depth signing, complete 2018 roster|publisher=Fox Sports|location=Australia|date=6 November 2017|access-date=24 January 2018}}</ref> At the end of the season he re- signed with the Dragons until the end of 2019.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.dragons.com.au/news/2018/11/02/dragons-re-sign-allgood-sign-two-more/|title=Dragons re-sign Allgood, sign two more|date=2018-11-02|website=St George Illawarra Dragons|publisher=National Rugby League|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181102055846/https://www.dragons.com.au/news/2018/11/02/dragons-re-sign-allgood-sign-two-more/|archive-date=2018-11-02|url-status=live|access-date=2018-11-02}}</ref>

Allgood made his debut for St George in round 16 of the [[2019 NRL season]] against the [[Melbourne Storm]] at [[WIN Stadium]].  In November 2019, it was announced that Allgood had been released by the club.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2019/01/23/2019-nrl-signings-player-transfers-and-contracts/#dragons/|title=NRL signings and player transfers|publisher=National Rugby League}}</ref>

===London Broncos===
On 31 January 2020, Allgood signed a contract to join the [[London Broncos]] for the 2020 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/mitch-allgood-joins-london/|title=Mitch Allgood joins London|website=loverugbyleague.com}}</ref>

==Representative career==
Allgood was named in the 2012 [[City New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales City]] team to replace [[Keith Galloway]] on the bench.

==Controversy==
In September 2013, Allgood was pulled over by police and charged with a drink driving offence.  It was alleged Allgood had been drinking with other Parramatta players as part of the club's Mad Monday celebrations.  On 18 December 2013 Allgood was handed a two-year good behaviour bond by the magistrate.<ref name="ABC News"/><ref name="The Daily Telegraph"/>
